active_paradigms.description: Formulate as constrained optimization: 52 variables (26 circle centers), radii computed analytically from positions. Objective: maximize sum of min(x, y, 1-x, 1-y, min_dist/2). Use trust-constr which handles nonlinear constraints robustly. Initialize with hexagonal lattice: place circles in rows with alternating offsets, row spacing = sqrt(3)/2 * estimated_diameter. Use multiple random perturbations of initial positions to escape local minima.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Circle center positions (52 variables), radii derived from constraints
active_paradigms.cautions: Trust-constr requires constraint Jacobians - use finite differences or define explicitly. Set tol=1e-6, maxiter=2000. The objective is non-smooth due to min() operations - may need smooth approximation. Initialize centers away from boundaries (use 0.05 to 0.95 range).

active_paradigms.description: Differential evolution is population-based global optimizer that handles non-convex objectives well. Define 52-variable problem with bounds [0.05, 0.95] for each coordinate. Objective: negative sum of computed radii (minimize to maximize). Handle constraints via penalty: add large penalty (e.g., 1000) times sum of constraint violations. Use strategy='best1bin', popsize=20, maxiter=500, mutation=(0.5, 1), recombination=0.7. Set seed=42 for reproducibility.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Global search over position space avoiding local minima
active_paradigms.cautions: DE doesn't natively support constraints - must use penalty or barrier methods. Population size affects runtime significantly. For 52 variables, popsize=15-25 is reasonable. May timeout if settings too aggressive - start conservative.

active_paradigms.idea: Geometric corner-first construction with SLSQP refinement
active_paradigms.description: Phase 1: Place 4 corner circles at (r,r), (1-r, r), (r, 1-r), (1-r, 1-r) with r≈0.15. Phase 2: Place 8 edge circles along each side between corners, equally spaced. Phase 3: Fill interior with hexagonal pattern (rows of 4-5 circles, offset by half-spacing). Phase 4: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP method to refine all positions simultaneously, starting from geometric arrangement. This combines geometric insight (corners/edges valuable) with numerical optimization.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Initial geometric placement + subsequent numerical refinement
active_paradigms.cautions: SLSQP needs smooth objective - approximate min() with soft-min: -log(sum(exp(-k*values)))/k. Geometric initialization must be valid (no overlaps) or SLSQP may fail. Corner radius ~0.15 allows edge circles r~0.08-0.1.
